text: Active recovery interventions lasting 6-10 minutes consistently show positive effects on subsequent athletic performance.
population: professional, collegiate, and competitive adult athletes

text: Blood lactate clearance rate is an unreliable marker of recovery status and should not be used as the primary indicator of recovery effectiveness.
population: professional, collegiate, and competitive adult athletes

text: Active recovery sessions produce positive psychological outcomes regardless of their physiological effectiveness on performance.
population: professional, collegiate, and competitive adult athletes

text: The optimal intensity level for active recovery sessions has not been reliably established in the literature.
population: professional, collegiate, and competitive adult athletes

text: Evidence for post-exercise active recovery improving subsequent athletic performance is weak overall, and active recovery may need to be individualized for effectiveness.
population: professional, collegiate, and competitive adult athletes
